blocks.original_text: It seemed a thousand years ago that Catelyn Stark had carried her infant son out of Riverrun, crossing the Tumblestone in a small boat to begin their journey north to Winterfell. And it was across the Tumblestone that they came home now, though the boy wore plate and mail in place of swaddling clothes.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn想起多年前抱infant Robb渡过Tumblestone北上，如今同一孩子穿plate and mail凯旋归来。
blocks.literary_note_zh: infant swaddling与plate/mail把Robb成长压缩成一次渡河；homecoming也是childhood终结。
blocks.background_zh: **Tumblestone crossing：** Catelyn曾从Riverrun带infant Robb去Winterfell；同一路线反向返回构成成长环。

blocks.original_text: Robb sat in the bow with Grey Wind, his hand resting on his direwolf s head as the rowers pulled at their oars. Theon Greyjoy was with him. Her uncle Brynden would come behind in the second boat, with the Greatjon and Lord Karstark.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb与Grey Wind坐船头，Theon同行，Brynden、Greatjon和Karstark乘后船。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn与披甲Robb渡河归Riverrun；胜利欢呼被Eddard之死、Hoster病危和她对战争起因的guilt压住。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: Catelyn took a place toward the stern. They shot down the Tumblestone, letting the strong current push them past the looming Wheel Tower. The splash and rumble of the great waterwheel within was a sound from her girlhood that brought a sad smile to Catelyn’s face. From the sandstone walls of the castle, soldiers and servants shouted down her name, and Robb’s, and “Winterfell!” From every rampart waved the banner of House Tully: a leaping trout, silver, against a rippling blue-and-red field. It was a stirring sight, yet it did not lift her heart. She wondered if indeed her heart would ever lift again. Oh, Ned . . .
blocks.explanation_zh: 童年waterwheel声、Tully banners与欢呼本应振奋，Eddard之死却使Catelyn无法真正高兴。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 公共victory景观无法穿透私人grief，Catelyn的双重身份从开章即冲突。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: Below the Wheel Tower, they made a wide turn and knifed through the churning water. The men put their backs into it. The wide arch of the Water Gate came into view, and she heard the creak of heavy chains as the great iron portcullis was winched upward. It rose slowly as they approached, and Catelyn saw that the lower half of it was red with rust. The bottom foot dripped brown mud on them as they passed underneath, the barbed spikes mere inches above their heads. Catelyn gazed up at the bars and wondered how deep the rust went and how well the portcullis would stand up to a ram and whether it ought to be replaced. Thoughts like that were seldom far from her mind these days.
blocks.explanation_zh: 船从锈蚀portcullis下进入Water Gate；Catelyn本能评估城防能否承受ram，战争思维已成为日常。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 看到rust便想ram说明战争已重塑她的attention，不再能单纯以归乡者观看城堡。
blocks.background_zh: **Water Gate：** Riverrun可由河道直接进入城内，portcullis锈蚀也说明长期防御维护问题。

blocks.original_text: They passed beneath the arch and under the walls, moving from sunlight to shadow and back into sunlight. Boats large and small were tied up all around them, secured to iron rings set in the stone. Her father’s guards waited on the water stair with her brother. Ser Edmure Tully was a stocky young man with a shaggy head of auburn hair and a fiery beard. His breastplate was scratched and dented from battle, his blue-and-red cloak stained by blood and smoke. At his side stood the Lord Tytos Blackwood, a hard pike of a man with close-cropped salt-and-pepper whiskers and a hook nose. His bright yellow armor was inlaid with jet in elaborate vine-and-leaf patterns, and a cloak sewn from raven feathers draped his thin shoulders. It had been Lord Tytos who led the sortie that plucked her brother from the Lannister camp.
blocks.explanation_zh: Edmure与救他出营的Tytos Blackwood在water stair迎接；二人armor都留下战斗痕迹。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn与披甲Robb渡河归Riverrun；胜利欢呼被Eddard之死、Hoster病危和她对战争起因的guilt压住。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “Bring them in,” Ser Edmure commanded. Three men scrambled down the stairs knee-deep in the water and pulled the boat close with long hooks. When Grey Wind bounded out, one of them dropped his pole and lurched back, stumbling and sitting down abruptly in the river. The others laughed, and the man got a sheepish look on his face. Theon Greyjoy vaulted over the side of the boat and lifted Catelyn by the waist, setting her on a dry step above him as water lapped around his boots.
blocks.explanation_zh: 仆役用hooks拉船，Grey Wind吓得一人跌入水；Theon把Catelyn抱上干燥台阶。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn与披甲Robb渡河归Riverrun；胜利欢呼被Eddard之死、Hoster病危和她对战争起因的guilt压住。

blocks.original_text: Edmure came down the steps to embrace her. “Sweet sister,” he murmured hoarsely. He had deep blue eyes and a mouth made for smiles, but he was not smiling now. He looked worn and tired, battered by battle and haggard from strain. His neck was bandaged where he had taken a wound. Catelyn hugged him fiercely.
blocks.explanation_zh: 负伤憔悴的Edmure拥抱Catelyn，姐弟重逢没有笑容。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn与披甲Robb渡河归Riverrun；胜利欢呼被Eddard之死、Hoster病危和她对战争起因的guilt压住。

blocks.original_text: “Your grief is mine, Cat,” he said when they broke apart. “When we heard about Lord Eddard . . . the Lannisters will pay, I swear it, you will have your vengeance.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Edmure分享她的grief，并发誓让Lannisters付出代价。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn与披甲Robb渡河归Riverrun；胜利欢呼被Eddard之死、Hoster病危和她对战争起因的guilt压住。

blocks.original_text: “Will that bring Ned back to me?” she said sharply. The wound was still too fresh for softer words. She could not think about Ned now. She would not. It would not do. She had to be strong. “All that will keep. I must see Father.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn尖锐问vengeance能否带回Ned，压下哀痛要求先见Hoster。
blocks.literary_note_zh: ‘bring Ned back’在本章反复成为反复仇论证核心：情绪合理不等于手段能实现目标。

blocks.original_text: “He awaits you in his solar,” Edmure said.
blocks.explanation_zh: Edmure说Hoster在solar等她。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn与披甲Robb渡河归Riverrun；胜利欢呼被Eddard之死、Hoster病危和她对战争起因的guilt压住。

blocks.original_text: “Lord Hoster is bedridden, my lady,” her father’s steward explained. When had that good man grown so old and grey? “He instructed me to bring you to him at once.”
blocks.explanation_zh: steward说明Hoster卧床，并要求立即带Catelyn去见。

blocks.original_text: “I’ll take her.” Edmure escorted her up the water stair and across the lower bailey, where Petyr Baelish and Brandon Stark had once crossed swords for her favor. The massive sandstone walls of the keep loomed above them. As they pushed through a door between two guardsmen in fish-crest helms, she asked, “How bad is he?” dreading the answer even as she said the words.
blocks.explanation_zh: Edmure陪她穿过曾见Petyr与Brandon决斗的bailey；Catelyn恐惧地问病情。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn与披甲Robb渡河归Riverrun；胜利欢呼被Eddard之死、Hoster病危和她对战争起因的guilt压住。
blocks.background_zh: **Petyr–Brandon duel：** Petyr少年时为Catelyn挑战其婚约对象Brandon，在此bailey附近败北。

blocks.original_text: Edmure’s look was somber. “He will not be with us long, the maesters say. The pain is . . . constant, and grievous.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Edmure坦白maesters认为Hoster时日无多且持续剧痛。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn与披甲Robb渡河归Riverrun；胜利欢呼被Eddard之死、Hoster病危和她对战争起因的guilt压住。

blocks.original_text: A blind rage filled her, a rage at all the world; at her brother Edmure and her sister Lysa, at the Lannisters, at the maesters, at Ned and her father and the monstrous gods who would take them both away from her. “You should have told me,” she said. “You should have sent word as soon as you knew.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn对亲友、maesters和gods同时愤怒，责怪Edmure没有早通知。
blocks.literary_note_zh: rage同时指向死者、亲属和gods，是失去控制后的广泛归责，不是逐项理性判断。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “He forbade it. He did not want his enemies to know that he was dying. With the realm so troubled, he feared that if the Lannisters suspected how frail he was . . . ”
blocks.explanation_zh: Edmure说Hoster禁止走漏病危消息，怕Lannisters利用Riverrun领主虚弱。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn与披甲Robb渡河归Riverrun；胜利欢呼被Eddard之死、Hoster病危和她对战争起因的guilt压住。

blocks.original_text: “ . . . they might attack?” Catelyn finished, hard. It was your doing, yours, a voice whispered inside her. If you had not taken it upon yourself to seize the dwarf . . .

blocks.original_text: The keep was three-sided, like Riverrun itself, and Lord Hoster’s solar was triangular as well, with a stone balcony that jutted out to the east like the prow of some great sandstone ship. From there the lord of the castle could look down on his walls and battlements, and beyond, to where the waters met. They had moved her father’s bed out onto the balcony. “He likes to sit in the sun and watch the rivers,” Edmure explained. “Father, see who I’ve brought. Cat has come to see you . . . ”
blocks.explanation_zh: 三角solar像Riverrun地形本身；Hoster的床被移到能看两河的sunlit balcony。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn探望临终Hoster；父亲在孩子团聚、Lysa缺席与Brynden拒婚旧怨之间断续回忆。

blocks.original_text: Hoster Tully had always been a big man; tall and broad in his youth, portly as he grew older. Now he seemed shrunken, the muscle and meat melted off his bones. Even his face sagged. The last time Catelyn had seen him, his hair and beard had been brown, well streaked with grey. Now they had gone white as snow.
blocks.explanation_zh: 曾高大魁梧的Hoster如今严重消瘦，头发胡须全白。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn探望临终Hoster；父亲在孩子团聚、Lysa缺席与Brynden拒婚旧怨之间断续回忆。

blocks.original_text: His eyes opened to the sound of Edmure’s voice. “Little cat,” he murmured in a voice thin and wispy and wracked by pain. “My little cat.” A tremulous smile touched his face as his hand groped for hers. “I watched for you . . . ”
blocks.explanation_zh: Hoster称她little cat，摸索女儿的手，说一直等她。
blocks.literary_note_zh: little cat使成年政治行动者暂时回到父亲眼中的child，强化代际循环。

blocks.original_text: “I shall leave you to talk,” her brother said, kissing their lord father gently on the brow before he withdrew.

blocks.original_text: Catelyn knelt and took her father’s hand in hers. It was a big hand, but fleshless now, the bones moving loosely under the skin, all the strength gone from it. “You should have told me,” she said. “A rider, a raven . . . ”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn握着失去力量的手，再次问为何不送rider或raven。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn探望临终Hoster；父亲在孩子团聚、Lysa缺席与Brynden拒婚旧怨之间断续回忆。

blocks.original_text: “Riders are taken, questioned,” he answered. “Ravens are brought down . . . ” A spasm of pain took him, and his fingers clutched hers hard. “The crabs are in my belly . . . pinching, always pinching. Day and night. They have fierce claws, the crabs. Maester Vyman makes me dreamwine, milk of the poppy . . . I sleep a lot . . . but I wanted to be awake to see you, when you came. I was afraid . . . when the Lannisters took your brother, the camps all around us . . . was afraid I would go, before I could see you again . . . I was afraid . . . ”
blocks.explanation_zh: Hoster说通信会被截；他用belly里的crabs形容持续疼痛，并说靠dreamwine和milk of the poppy睡眠，只怕死前见不到孩子。
blocks.literary_note_zh: crabs是Hoster对腹痛的体验性比喻，文本不提供可确定的现代诊断。
blocks.background_zh: **Hoster illness：** 严重消瘦、持续腹痛并用opiates缓解；当前文本不提供明确疾病名。

blocks.original_text: “I’m here, Father,” she said. “With Robb, my son. He’ll want to see you too.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn安慰自己已到，并带来Robb。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn探望临终Hoster；父亲在孩子团聚、Lysa缺席与Brynden拒婚旧怨之间断续回忆。

blocks.original_text: “Your boy,” he whispered. “He had my eyes, I remember . . . ”

blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn探望临终Hoster；父亲在孩子团聚、Lysa缺席与Brynden拒婚旧怨之间断续回忆。

blocks.original_text: Lord Hoster smiled. “I saw. Last night, when it began, I told them . . . had to see. They carried me to the gatehouse . . . watched from the battlements. Ah, that was beautiful . . . the torches came in a wave, I could hear the cries floating across the river . . . sweet cries . . . when that siege tower went up, gods . . . would have died then, and glad, if only I could have seen you children first. Was it your boy who did it? Was it your Robb?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Hoster昨夜被抬上battlements观看夜袭，觉得Lannister喊声和siege tower燃烧很美，并急问是否Robb所为。
blocks.literary_note_zh: sweet cries显示Hoster把解除围城与敌军痛苦绑定；病危并未消除封建战争情绪。
blocks.background_zh: **Riverrun relief：** Robb/Blackfish夜袭三营，城内Tytos Blackwood sortie解救Edmure并打破围城。

blocks.original_text: “Yes,” Catelyn said, fiercely proud. “It was Robb . . . and Brynden. Your brother is here as well, my lord.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn骄傲确认是Robb与Brynden共同完成，Brynden也回来了。

blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn探望临终Hoster；父亲在孩子团聚、Lysa缺席与Brynden拒婚旧怨之间断续回忆。

I saw him tear one man’s arm from his shoulder, and their horses went mad at the scent of him. I couldn’t tell you how many men were thrown—”

blocks.original_text: She found Robb beneath the green canopy of leaves, surrounded by tall redwoods and great old elms, kneeling before the heart tree, a slender weirwood with a face more sad than fierce. His longsword was before him, the point thrust in the earth, his gloved hands clasped around the hilt. Around him others knelt: Greatjon Umber, Rickard Karstark, Maege Mormont, Galbart Glover, and more. Even Tytos Blackwood was among them, the great raven cloak fanned out behind him. These are the ones who keep the old gods, she realized. She asked herself what gods she kept these days, and could not find an answer.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb在heart tree前与守old gods的Northern lords祈祷；Catelyn已说不清自己还信何种gods。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 守old gods者围绕Robb祈祷，悄然勾勒即将形成的North政治共同体。
blocks.background_zh: **old gods group：** 祈祷者多来自North或Blackwood传统；Catelyn出身Tully，通常敬奉Faith of the Seven。

blocks.original_text: It would not do to disturb them at their prayers. The gods must have their due . . . even cruel gods who would take Ned from her, and her lord father as well. So Catelyn waited. The river wind moved through the high branches, and she could see the Wheel Tower to her right, ivy crawling up its side. As she stood there, all the memories came flooding back to her. Her father had taught her to ride amongst these trees, and that was the elm that Edmure had fallen from when he broke his arm, and over there, beneath that bower, she and Lysa had played at kissing with Petyr.
blocks.explanation_zh: 她等待祈祷结束，童年骑马、Edmure摔伤及与Lysa、Petyr玩耍的记忆涌回。

blocks.original_text: The war council convened in the Great Hall, at four long trestle tables arranged in a broken square. Lord Hoster was too weak to attend, asleep on his balcony, dreaming of the sun on the rivers of his youth. Edmure sat in the high seat of the Tullys, with Brynden Blackfish at his side, and his father’s bannermen arrayed to right and left and along the side tables. Word of the victory at Riverrun had spread to the fugitive lords of the Trident, drawing them back. Karyl Vance came in, a lord now, his father dead beneath the Golden Tooth. Ser Marq Piper was with him, and they brought a Darry, Ser Raymun’s son, a lad no older than Bran. Lord Jonos Bracken arrived from the ruins of Stone Hedge, glowering and blustering, and took a seat as far from Tytos Blackwood as the tables would permit.
blocks.explanation_zh: war council在Great Hall举行；Hoster缺席，riverlords因Riverrun胜利陆续回归，其中多家已承受领主死亡或领地毁坏。
blocks.literary_note_zh: riverlords回归并非完整恢复：dead fathers、ruined seats和child heirs把战争损失带进议事厅。
blocks.background_zh: **riverlords return：** 解围后此前逃散或领地被毁的Trident lords重新集结，但伤亡与旧家仇仍在。

blocks.original_text: The northern lords sat opposite, with Catelyn and Robb facing her brother across the tables. They were fewer. The Greatjon sat at Robb’s left hand, and then Theon Greyjoy; Galbart Glover and Lady Mormont were to the right of Catelyn. Lord Rickard Karstark, gaunt and hollow-eyed in his grief, took his seat like a man in a nightmare, his long beard uncombed and unwashed. He had left two sons dead in the Whispering Wood, and there was no word of the third, his eldest, who had led the Karstark spears against Tywin Lannister on the Green Fork.
blocks.explanation_zh: Northern lords人数较少；Karstark因两个儿子战死、长子失联而形如梦游。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robb祈祷后召集war council；Riverrun童年记忆与诸侯战争损失一起进入Great Hall。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: The arguing raged on late into the night. Each lord had a right to speak, and speak they did . . . and shout, and curse, and reason, and cajole, and jest, and bargain, and slam tankards on the table, and threaten, and walk out, and return sullen or smiling. Catelyn sat and listened to it all.
blocks.explanation_zh: 争论持续到深夜，各lord按权利发言、叫骂、交易、离席又返回，Catelyn始终倾听。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 长串动词表现feudal council不是现代投票，而是身份、声量、交易与武力威望共同作用。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: Roose Bolton had re-formed the battered remnants of their other host at the mouth of the causeway. Ser Helman Tallhart and Walder Frey still held the Twins. Lord Tywin’s army had crossed the Trident, and was making for Harrenhal. And there were two kings in the realm. Two kings, and no agreement.
blocks.explanation_zh: 情报显示Bolton重组残军、Frey守Twins、Tywin去Harrenhal，而realm已有两位kings却无共同立场。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 诸侯争论攻守、Renly/Stannis succession和议和；Robb坚持right却无法确定合法道路。
blocks.background_zh: **current map：** Bolton在causeway、Frey守Twins、Tywin向Harrenhal；Robb控制Riverrun但政治目标未定。

blocks.original_text: Many of the lords bannermen wanted to march on Harrenhal at once, to meet Lord Tywin and end Lannister power for all time. Young, hot-tempered Marq Piper urged a strike west at Casterly Rock instead. Still others counseled patience. Riverrun sat athwart the Lannister supply lines, Jason Mallister pointed out; let them bide their time, denying Lord Tywin fresh levies and provisions while they strengthened their defenses and rested their weary troops. Lord Blackwood would have none of it. They should finish the work they began in the Whispering Wood. March to Harrenhal and bring Roose Bolton’s army down as well. What Blackwood urged, Bracken opposed, as ever; Lord Jonos Bracken rose to insist they ought pledge their fealty to King Renly, and move south to join their might to his.
blocks.explanation_zh: 诸侯分别主张攻Harrenhal、袭Casterly Rock、守Riverrun断补给或投Renly；Blackwood与Bracken依旧相互反对。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 诸侯争论攻守、Renly/Stannis succession和议和；Robb坚持right却无法确定合法道路。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “Renly is not the king,” Robb said. It was the first time her son had spoken. Like his father, he knew how to listen.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb首次开口，否认Renly已经是合法king，展现与Eddard相似的倾听习惯。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robb先听后说复制Eddard治理风格，形式上的克制提高发言重量。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “You cannot mean to hold to Joffrey, my lord,” Galbart Glover said. “He put your father to death.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Galbart提醒Joffrey处决了Eddard，不能继续效忠。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 诸侯争论攻守、Renly/Stannis succession和议和；Robb坚持right却无法确定合法道路。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “That makes him evil,” Robb replied. “I do not know that it makes Renly king. Joffrey is still Robert’s eldest trueborn son, so the throne is rightfully his by all the laws of the realm. Were he to die, and I mean to see that he does, he has a younger brother. Tommen is next in line after Joffrey.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb区分Joffrey的evil与succession law：若他是Robert trueborn eldest则合法，死后也应由Tommen继承。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段‘trueborn’是Robb当前认知；读者已知Eddard调查所得，因此其法律推理建立在信息缺口上。
blocks.background_zh: **succession rule：** 按Robb掌握的信息，Robert的trueborn sons优先于brothers，brothers又按长幼排序。

blocks.original_text: “Tommen is no less a Lannister,” Ser Marq Piper snapped.
blocks.explanation_zh: Marq Piper说Tommen同样是Lannister。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 诸侯争论攻守、Renly/Stannis succession和议和；Robb坚持right却无法确定合法道路。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “As you say,” said Robb, troubled. “Yet if neither one is king, still, how could it be Lord Renly? He’s Robert’s younger brother. Bran can’t be Lord of Winterfell before me, and Renly can’t be king before Lord Stannis.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb仍追问为何应跳过Robert的兄长Stannis让弟弟Renly继承，并用自己与Bran次序类比。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 诸侯争论攻守、Renly/Stannis succession和议和；Robb坚持right却无法确定合法道路。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: Lady Mormont agreed. “Lord Stannis has the better claim.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Maege同意Stannis的claim更强。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 诸侯争论攻守、Renly/Stannis succession和议和；Robb坚持right却无法确定合法道路。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “Renly is crowned,” said Marq Piper. “Highgarden and Storm’s End support his claim, and the Dornishmen will not be laggardly. If Winterfell and Riverrun add their strength to his, he will have five of the seven great houses behind him. Six, if the Arryns bestir themselves! Six against the Rock! My lords, within the year, we will have all their heads on pikes, the queen and the boy king, Lord Tywin, the Imp, the Kingslayer, Ser Kevan, all of them! That is what we shall win if we join with King Renly. What does Lord Stannis have against that, that we should cast it all aside?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Marq以Renly已经crowned且可能联合五六great houses论证实力，承诺击败所有Lannisters，反问Stannis除了right还有什么。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Marq对比的是power coalition与legal right；两种king标准从此公开冲突。
blocks.background_zh: **great houses arithmetic：** Marq把Baratheon/Tyrell/Stark/Tully及可能的Arryn/Dorne视为联盟资源，属于政治预测。

blocks.original_text: “The right,” said Robb stubbornly. Catelyn thought he sounded eerily like his father as he said it.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb固执回答：The right；Catelyn听见Eddard般的语气。
blocks.literary_note_zh: The right仅两词，既显原则也暴露Robb尚无可实行方案。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “So you mean us to declare for Stannis?” asked Edmure.
blocks.explanation_zh: Edmure问是否因此宣布支持Stannis。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 诸侯争论攻守、Renly/Stannis succession和议和；Robb坚持right却无法确定合法道路。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “I don’t know,” said Robb. “I prayed to know what to do, but the gods did not answer. The Lannisters killed my father for a traitor, and we know that was a lie, but if Joffrey is the lawful king and we fight against him, we will be traitors.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb承认不知道；gods未回答，而若Joffrey合法，他们反抗就会成为traitors。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 诸侯争论攻守、Renly/Stannis succession和议和；Robb坚持right却无法确定合法道路。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “My lord father would urge caution,” aged Ser Stevron said, with the weaselly smile of a Frey. “Wait, let these two kings play their game of thrones. When they are done fighting, we can bend our knees to the victor, or oppose him, as we choose. With Renly arming, likely Lord Tywin would welcome a truce . . . and the safe return of his son. Noble lords, allow me to go to him at Harrenhal and arrange good terms and ransoms . . . ”
blocks.explanation_zh: Stevron建议等待两王相争后再选胜者，并提议拿Jaime与Tywin议和赎俘。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Stevron的wait-and-ransom有现实理性，也服务Frey避免承担风险的利益。
blocks.background_zh: **ransom leverage：** Jaime与其他Lannister prisoners可交换Sansa、Arya或换取停战，但执行取决于双方条件。

blocks.original_text: A roar of outrage drowned out his voice. “Craven!” the Greatjon thundered. “Begging for a truce will make us seem weak,” declared Lady Mormont. “Ransoms be damned, we must not give up the Kingslayer,” shouted Rickard Karstark.
blocks.explanation_zh: 诸侯以craven、示弱和不能交还Kingslayer等呼喊淹没提议。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 诸侯争论攻守、Renly/Stannis succession和议和；Robb坚持right却无法确定合法道路。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “Why not a peace?” Catelyn asked.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn提出为何不能peace。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn以living family与战争原始目标争取peace，短暂说服众人，却被复仇、安全与合法性疑虑打断。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: The lords looked at her, but it was Robb’s eyes she felt, his and his alone. “My lady, they murdered my lord father, your husband,” he said grimly. He unsheathed his longsword and laid it on the table before him, the bright steel on the rough wood. “This is the only peace I have for Lannisters.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb拔剑置于桌上，说Lannisters杀父，这把剑是自己唯一提供的和平。
blocks.literary_note_zh: sword on table把peace词义改写成submission through violence，随后Catelyn争取把它恢复为停止死亡。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: The Greatjon bellowed his approval, and other men added their voices, shouting and drawing swords and pounding their fists on the table. Catelyn waited until they had quieted. “My lords,” she said then, “Lord Eddard was your liege, but I shared his bed and bore his children. Do you think I love him any less than you?” Her voice almost broke with her grief, but Catelyn took a long breath and steadied herself. “Robb, if that sword could bring him back, I should never let you sheathe it until Ned stood at my side once more . . . but he is gone, and hundred Whispering Woods will not change that. Ned is gone, and Daryn Hornwood, and Lord Karstark’s valiant sons, and many other good men besides, and none of them will return to us. Must we have more deaths still?”
blocks.explanation_zh: 众人欢呼后Catelyn以妻子和母亲身份反问谁更爱Ned；她说再多victories也不能复活死者，只会增加死亡。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn不是否定Ned价值，而以自己更亲密的grief取得反对报复升级的发言资格。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “You are a woman, my lady,” the Greatjon rumbled in his deep voice. “Women do not understand these things.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Greatjon以她是woman为由否定其判断。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn以living family与战争原始目标争取peace，短暂说服众人，却被复仇、安全与合法性疑虑打断。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “You are the gentle sex,” said Lord Karstark, with the lines of grief fresh on his face. “A man has a need for vengeance.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Karstark称women是gentle sex，而men需要vengeance。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn以living family与战争原始目标争取peace，短暂说服众人，却被复仇、安全与合法性疑虑打断。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “Give me Cersei Lannister, Lord Karstark, and you would see how gentle a woman can be,” Catelyn replied. “Perhaps I do not understand tactics and strategy . . . but I understand futility. We went to war when Lannister armies were ravaging the riverlands, and Ned was a prisoner, falsely accused of treason. We fought to defend ourselves, and to win my lord’s freedom.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn反击若交出Cersei便能见识她是否gentle；她承认不擅tactics，却理解战争目标的futility。
blocks.literary_note_zh: gentle sex被她用Cersei反例反击；核心仍是futility而非证明女性更暴力。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “Well, the one is done, and the other forever beyond our reach. I will mourn for Ned until the end of my days, but I must think of the living. I want my daughters back, and the queen holds them still. If I must trade our four Lannisters for their two Starks, I will call that a bargain and thank the gods. I want you safe, Robb, ruling at Winterfell from your father’s seat. I want you to live your life, to kiss a girl and wed a woman and father a son. I want to write an end to this. I want to go home, my lords, and weep for my husband.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 她说最初目标之一已完成、救Ned已不可能；希望用四名Lannisters换回女儿，让Robb回Winterfell生活、成婚、生子，自己回家哀悼。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 连续I want把外交目标还原为具体living people和未被战争吞掉的future。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: The hall was very quiet when Catelyn finished speaking.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn说完后hall完全安静，说明论证短暂触及众人。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn以living family与战争原始目标争取peace，短暂说服众人，却被复仇、安全与合法性疑虑打断。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “Peace,” said her uncle Brynden. “Peace is sweet, my lady . . . but on what terms? It is no good hammering your sword into a plowshare if you must forge it again on the morrow.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Brynden说peace很甜，却必须有可持续条款，否则plowshare很快又得锻回sword。
blocks.literary_note_zh: plowshare意象说明peace必须解决安全困境；单方解除武装会使下一轮战争更危险。
blocks.background_zh: **plowshare：** 把武器锻成农具象征和平；Brynden强调若敌意结构未解，解除武装只是短暂。

blocks.original_text: “What did Torrhen and my Eddard die for, if I am to return to Karhold with nothing but their bones?” asked Rickard Karstark.
blocks.explanation_zh: Karstark问若只带儿子尸骨回Karhold，他们究竟为何而死。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn以living family与战争原始目标争取peace，短暂说服众人，却被复仇、安全与合法性疑虑打断。

blocks.original_text: “Aye,” said Lord Bracken. “Gregor Clegane laid waste to my fields, slaughtered my smallfolk, and left Stone Hedge a smoking ruin. Am I now to bend the knee to the ones who sent him? What have we fought for, if we are to put all back as it was before?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bracken说Gregor毁田杀民、Stone Hedge成废墟，不能让一切恢复原状再向责任方屈膝。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn以living family与战争原始目标争取peace，短暂说服众人，却被复仇、安全与合法性疑虑打断。

blocks.original_text: Lord Blackwood agreed, to Catelyn’s surprise and dismay. “And if we do make peace with King Joffrey, are we not then traitors to King Renly? What if the stag should prevail against the lion, where would that leave us?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Blackwood罕见同意Bracken，并担心与Joffrey议和会在Renly胜出后成为traitors。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn以living family与战争原始目标争取peace，短暂说服众人，却被复仇、安全与合法性疑虑打断。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “Whatever you may decide for yourselves, I shall never call a Lannister my king,” declared Marq Piper.
blocks.explanation_zh: Marq宣布无论别人如何决定，自己永不称Lannister为king。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn以living family与战争原始目标争取peace，短暂说服众人，却被复仇、安全与合法性疑虑打断。

blocks.original_text: “Nor I!” yelled the little Darry boy. “I never will!”
blocks.explanation_zh: 年幼Darry也激动响应。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn以living family与战争原始目标争取peace，短暂说服众人，却被复仇、安全与合法性疑虑打断。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: Again the shouting began. Catelyn sat despairing. She had come so close, she thought. They had almost listened, almost . . . but the moment was gone. There would be no peace, no chance to heal, no safety. She looked at her son, watched him as he listened to the lords debate, frowning, troubled, yet wedded to his war. He had pledged himself to marry a daughter of Walder Frey, but she saw his true bride plain before her now: the sword he had laid on the table.
blocks.explanation_zh: 争吵重新爆发，Catelyn绝望地看见peace时机消失；她把Robb真正的bride认作桌上的sword。
blocks.literary_note_zh: true bride=sword把Walder Frey婚约与war commitment相对照，预示政治成人身份吞噬私人生活。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: Catelyn was thinking of her girls, wondering if she would ever see them again, when the Greatjon lurched to his feet.
blocks.explanation_zh: 她正思念女儿，Greatjon突然起身。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Greatjon跳出南方王位选择，拥立Robb为King in the North，North与riverlords共同宣誓。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “MY LORDS!” he shouted, his voice booming off the rafters. “Here is what I say to these two kings!” He spat. “ Renly Baratheon is nothing to me, nor Stannis neither. Why should they rule over me and mine, from some flowery seat in Highgarden or Dorne? What do they know of the Wall or the wolfswood or the barrows of the First Men? Even their gods are wrong. The Others take the Lannisters too, I’ve had a bellyful of them.” He reached back over his shoulder and drew his immense two-handed greatsword. “Why shouldn’t we rule ourselves again? It was the dragons we married, and the dragons are all dead!” He pointed at Robb with the blade. “There sits the only king I mean to bow my knee to, m’lords,” he thundered. “The King in the North!”
blocks.explanation_zh: Greatjon拒绝Renly、Stannis和Lannisters，主张North恢复dragon conquest前的自主，并称Robb为King in the North。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Greatjon不在两位Baratheon中选择，而改变问题：North为何仍需southron king。
blocks.background_zh: **pre-Conquest kingship：** Aegon征服前三百多年，North由Kings of Winter统治；Greatjon主张dragon dynasty消失后恢复独立。

blocks.original_text: And he knelt, and laid his sword at her son’s feet.
blocks.explanation_zh: Greatjon跪下，把greatsword放在Robb脚边。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Greatjon跳出南方王位选择，拥立Robb为King in the North，North与riverlords共同宣誓。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “I’ll have peace on those terms,” Lord Karstark said. “They can keep their red castle and their iron chair as well.” He eased his longsword from its scabbard. “The King in the North!” he said, kneeling beside the Greatjon.
blocks.explanation_zh: Karstark接受这种peace，拒绝Red Keep与Iron Throne，也跪下称王。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Greatjon跳出南方王位选择，拥立Robb为King in the North，North与riverlords共同宣誓。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: Maege Mormont stood. “The King of Winter!” she declared, and laid her spiked mace beside the swords. And the river lords were rising too, Blackwood and Bracken and Mallister, houses who had never been ruled from Winterfell, yet Catelyn watched them rise and draw their blades, bending their knees and shouting the old words that had not been heard in the realm for more than three hundred years, since Aegon the Dragon had come to make the Seven Kingdoms one . . . yet now were heard again, ringing from the timbers of her father’s hall:
blocks.explanation_zh: Maege称King of Winter；连从未受Winterfell统治的riverlords也起身宣誓，复活三百多年未闻的旧称号。
blocks.literary_note_zh: riverlords也宣誓最关键，因为他们此前不受Winterfell统治；这一刻是新联盟创造，不只是旧North传统恢复。
blocks.background_zh: **new realm：** riverlords加入意味着拥立不只是North内部复古，而是跨Trident的新政治共同体。

blocks.original_text: “The King in the North!”
blocks.explanation_zh: 众人第一次齐喊King in the North。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Greatjon跳出南方王位选择，拥立Robb为King in the North，North与riverlords共同宣誓。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “The King in the North!”
blocks.explanation_zh: 呼喊继续扩大。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Greatjon跳出南方王位选择，拥立Robb为King in the North，North与riverlords共同宣誓。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。

blocks.original_text: “THE KING IN THE NORTH!”
blocks.explanation_zh: 最终以全大写的集体宣告达到政治与情绪高潮。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 三次重复逐渐放大为集体政治现实；情绪一致暂时覆盖前一刻的路线分裂。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须展开的设定；重点在Riverrun family history、复仇与peace目标、succession legitimacy及Robb的新政治身份。
